summary.chief_complaint: Acute worsening of dyspnea, wheezing, and increased sputum production
summary.history_of_present_illness: 76-year-old male with severe COPD (FEV1 28% predicted) presenting with acute exacerbation. Increased dyspnea at rest, productive cough with purulent sputum, unable to complete sentences. On 3L home oxygen.
summary.past_medical_history: Severe COPD (GOLD IV), Cor pulmonale, CHF, OSA on CPAP, Former smoker 60 pack-years
summary.medications_on_admission: Fluticasone/salmeterol 500/50 BID, Tiotropium 18mcg daily, Home O2 3L/min, Furosemide 40mg daily
summary.physical_examination: BP 148/92, HR 108, RR 32, SpO2 82% on 3L NC. Accessory muscle use, pursed lip breathing. Diffuse expiratory wheezes and rhonchi bilaterally. JVD present. Bilateral pedal edema 2+.
summary.lab_results: ABG: pH 7.28, pCO2 68, pO2 52, HCO3 31. WBC 15.8K, Procalcitonin 1.2 ng/mL. BNP 650 pg/mL. Sputum culture: Pseudomonas aeruginosa.
summary.imaging_results: CXR: Hyperinflated lungs, flattened diaphragms, RLL infiltrate. CT chest: Severe emphysema, RLL consolidation, pulmonary artery enlargement suggesting pulmonary hypertension.
summary.hospital_course: BiPAP initiated with improvement in respiratory acidosis. IV methylprednisolone and nebulized bronchodilators. Piperacillin-tazobactam for Pseudomonas pneumonia. Diuresis for cor pulmonale. Improved over 5 days, weaned to nasal cannula. Pulmonary rehab initiated. Advanced care planning discussion held.
summary.discharge_diagnosis: Acute exacerbation of COPD with respiratory failure, Pseudomonas pneumonia, Cor pulmonale, Pulmonary hypertension
summary.discharge_medications: Fluticasone/salmeterol 500/50 BID, Tiotropium 18mcg daily, Prednisone 40mg taper over 10 days, Levofloxacin 750mg daily x 7 more days, Furosemide 60mg daily, Home O2 4L/min continuous
summary.follow_up_instructions: Pulmonology 1 week. Pulmonary rehab enrollment. Sleep medicine for CPAP reassessment. Goals of care documentation.

summary.chief_complaint: Severe headache, neck stiffness, fever, and photophobia for 12 hours
summary.history_of_present_illness: 28-year-old female presenting with sudden onset severe thunderclap headache rated 10/10, neck stiffness, fever 39.5°C, photophobia, and vomiting. Reports recent sinusitis treated with amoxicillin. College student living in dormitory.
summary.past_medical_history: Recurrent sinusitis, no surgical history, vaccinations up to date including meningococcal
summary.physical_examination: BP 135/85, HR 112, Temp 39.5°C, RR 22. Ill-appearing, photophobic. Positive Kernig and Brudzinski signs. Nuchal rigidity. No papilledema on fundoscopy. No focal neurologic deficits. Petechial rash on trunk.
summary.lab_results: WBC: 18.5K with left shift, CRP: 15.2, Procalcitonin: 8.5. CSF: WBC 2200 (95% PMNs), Glucose 22 (serum 105), Protein 280. CSF gram stain: Gram-positive diplococci. Blood cultures: Positive for Streptococcus pneumoniae.
summary.imaging_results: CT Head (pre-LP): No mass effect, no hemorrhage. MRI Brain: Meningeal enhancement, no abscess or ventriculitis.
summary.hospital_course: Empiric IV ceftriaxone and vancomycin started within 30 minutes, dexamethasone given before antibiotics. CSF culture confirmed S. pneumoniae (penicillin-sensitive). Narrowed to ceftriaxone. Dexamethasone continued x 4 days. Close contacts notified but no prophylaxis needed for pneumococcal. Headache gradually improved. No complications, hearing screen normal.
summary.discharge_medications: Ceftriaxone 2g IV daily via PICC (complete 14-day course), Acetaminophen 1000mg Q6H PRN, Ondansetron 4mg PRN
summary.follow_up_instructions: Infectious disease follow-up 1 week. Repeat hearing test in 4 weeks. PICC line care education. ID to determine when IV antibiotics can stop. Pneumococcal vaccine review.

summary.chief_complaint: Progressive fatigue, night sweats, and painless neck swelling for 6 weeks
summary.history_of_present_illness: 55-year-old male presenting with B symptoms (fevers, night sweats, 10% weight loss). Noticed progressive painless cervical lymphadenopathy. Reports pruritus after showering. No cough, dyspnea, or bone pain.
summary.physical_examination: BP 130/78, HR 80, Temp 38.2°C. Multiple enlarged, rubbery, non-tender cervical, axillary, and inguinal lymph nodes (largest 4cm cervical). Splenomegaly 5cm below costal margin. No hepatomegaly.
summary.lab_results: CBC: WBC 12.5K, Hgb 10.5, Platelets 180K. ESR: 72, LDH: 480, Albumin: 3.0. B2-microglobulin: 4.2. HIV: negative. Hepatitis panel: negative.
summary.imaging_results: PET-CT: Diffuse FDG-avid lymphadenopathy above and below the diaphragm with splenic involvement. Multiple nodes >2cm. No extranodal disease.
summary.hospital_course: Excisional biopsy of cervical node: Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma, nodular sclerosis subtype. Staging: Stage IIIB. Bone marrow biopsy: No involvement. Port-a-cath placed. First cycle of ABVD chemotherapy administered. Pre-chemo fertility counseling and sperm banking completed. PFTs baseline obtained for bleomycin monitoring. Tolerated first cycle well with mild nausea managed with ondansetron.
summary.discharge_diagnosis: Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma, Nodular Sclerosis, Stage IIIB
summary.discharge_medications: Ondansetron 8mg Q8H PRN, Dexamethasone 4mg BID x 3 days post-chemo, Filgrastim 5mcg/kg starting day 5 post-chemo, Acyclovir 400mg BID prophylaxis, Rosuvastatin 20mg daily
summary.follow_up_instructions: Oncology 2 weeks for cycle 2. CBC weekly. Report fever >38°C immediately. Avoid live vaccines. PFTs before each cycle.

summary.chief_complaint: Palpitations, dizziness, and syncope at home
summary.history_of_present_illness: 65-year-old female with history of hypertrophic cardiomyopathy presenting after syncopal episode at home preceded by palpitations. Family witnessed event lasting ~30 seconds with spontaneous recovery. No seizure activity.
summary.past_medical_history: Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (septal thickness 22mm), family history of sudden cardiac death (brother at age 42), Paroxysmal atrial fibrillation
summary.medications_on_admission: Metoprolol succinate 100mg daily, Apixaban 5mg BID, Disopyramide 150mg BID
summary.physical_examination: BP 118/72, HR 68, RR 16. Prominent systolic murmur at LLSB that increases with Valsalva. Bisferiens pulse. JVP prominent 'a' wave. No edema.
summary.lab_results: Troponin I: 0.08 (mildly elevated), BNP: 320, Electrolytes normal, Magnesium 1.8
summary.imaging_results: ECG: NSR with LVH, deep Q waves in lateral leads. Echocardiogram: Septal thickness 24mm, SAM of mitral valve, LVOT gradient 65mmHg at rest. Cardiac MRI: Late gadolinium enhancement in septum (fibrosis). Holter: 8-beat run of NSVT at 180bpm.
summary.hospital_course: Risk stratification for sudden cardiac death: Multiple risk factors (family hx SCD, syncope, NSVT, septal thickness >24mm, LGE on MRI). HCM Risk-SCD score: 8.2% - high risk. ICD implantation performed without complications. EP study demonstrated inducible VT. Device interrogation normal post-implant. Continued medical therapy.
summary.discharge_diagnosis: Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy with high-risk features, Syncope secondary to ventricular tachycardia, ICD implantation
summary.discharge_medications: Metoprolol succinate 100mg daily, Disopyramide 150mg BID, Apixaban 5mg BID, Acetaminophen 500mg Q6H PRN
summary.follow_up_instructions: Cardiology/EP 2 weeks for device check. No driving for 6 months per guidelines. Activity restrictions per HCM guidelines. Genetic counseling referral for family screening. Medical alert bracelet for ICD.

summary.chief_complaint: Suicidal ideation with plan, insomnia, and severe depressive symptoms for 3 weeks
summary.history_of_present_illness: 42-year-old male presenting via ED with worsening depression, suicidal ideation with plan (medication overdose). Recent job loss and divorce finalized 1 month ago. Reports 3-week history of insomnia, anhedonia, guilt, poor concentration, and 15-lb weight loss. Stopped taking medications 2 months ago.
summary.past_medical_history: Major depressive disorder (3 prior episodes), Generalized anxiety disorder, Prior suicide attempt at age 28 (overdose)
summary.medications_on_admission: Previously on Sertraline 150mg (stopped 2 months ago), Clonazepam 0.5mg PRN (stopped)
summary.physical_examination: BP 128/82, HR 88. PHQ-9: 24 (severe). Appears disheveled, psychomotor retardation, flat affect, tearful. Reports auditory hallucinations (derogatory voices). Denies visual hallucinations. Oriented x3. No focal deficits.
summary.lab_results: TSH: 2.1 (normal), CBC normal, CMP normal, Urine drug screen: negative, Blood alcohol: 0, Vitamin B12: normal, Folate: normal
summary.hospital_course: Admitted to inpatient psychiatry on 1:1 observation. Restarted antidepressant therapy with venlafaxine (cross-taper from sertraline). Low-dose quetiapine added for psychotic features and sleep. Individual and group therapy initiated. Safety planning completed. Social work assessment for housing and financial resources. Suicidal ideation resolved by day 3. Sleep improving with medication.
summary.discharge_diagnosis: Major depressive disorder, recurrent, severe with psychotic features. Suicidal ideation (resolved).
summary.discharge_medications: Venlafaxine XR 150mg daily, Quetiapine 200mg at bedtime, Hydroxyzine 25mg BID PRN anxiety
summary.follow_up_instructions: Psychiatry 3 days post-discharge. Intensive outpatient program enrollment. Crisis hotline information provided (988). Safety plan reviewed with patient and emergency contact. Weekly therapy appointments scheduled.

summary.chief_complaint: Worsening right lower quadrant pain, fever, and vomiting for 48 hours
summary.history_of_present_illness: 50-year-old female presenting with 48 hours of progressive RLQ pain initially periumbilical then migrating, associated with anorexia, nausea, vomiting, and fever. Pain now 9/10.
summary.past_medical_history: Hypertension, Type 2 DM, Previous cesarean section x 2
summary.physical_examination: BP 130/85, HR 108, Temp 38.8°C, RR 20. RLQ tenderness with rebound and guarding. Positive Rovsing sign. Psoas sign positive. Decreased bowel sounds.
summary.lab_results: WBC: 18.2K with 88% PMNs, CRP: 14.5, Lipase normal, Urinalysis: normal, HCG: negative, Lactate: 2.1
summary.imaging_results: CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast: Dilated appendix (14mm) with appendicolith, periappendiceal fat stranding, and contained perforation with 4cm abscess. No free air.
summary.hospital_course: Initially managed with IV antibiotics (piperacillin-tazobactam) and IR-guided percutaneous drainage of abscess (40mL purulent fluid). Clinical improvement over 48 hours. Interval laparoscopic appendectomy performed on day 3. Pathology showed perforated appendicitis with periappendiceal abscess, no malignancy. Tolerating diet post-op. Drain removed.
summary.discharge_medications: Amoxicillin-clavulanate 875mg BID x 7 days, Metformin 1000mg BID, Lisinopril 20mg daily, Acetaminophen 1000mg Q6H PRN, Ondansetron 4mg PRN
summary.follow_up_instructions: General surgery 2 weeks for wound check. Resume normal diet gradually. Activity restrictions 4 weeks. Return if fever, wound drainage, or increasing pain.

summary.chief_complaint: Fever, productive cough, and progressive shortness of breath for 5 days
summary.history_of_present_illness: 69-year-old male presenting with community-acquired pneumonia not responding to outpatient azithromycin. Reports high fever, rigors, productive cough with rust-colored sputum, pleuritic chest pain, and worsening dyspnea.
summary.past_medical_history: COPD (FEV1 55%), CHF (EF 40%), Type 2 DM, CKD Stage 3a
summary.medications_on_admission: Fluticasone/vilanterol 100/25 daily, Metoprolol 25mg BID, Furosemide 20mg daily, Metformin 500mg BID, Lisinopril 10mg daily
summary.physical_examination: BP 95/58, HR 112, RR 28, Temp 39.8°C, SpO2 87% on RA. Ill-appearing, rigors. Bronchial breath sounds RLL with egophony. Crackles bilaterally. Tachycardic, regular.
summary.lab_results: WBC: 22.5K, Procalcitonin: 12.8, CRP: 28, Lactate: 3.2, Creatinine: 2.4 (baseline 1.5), BUN: 42. Blood cultures: 2/2 positive for S. pneumoniae. Sputum: Gram-positive diplococci. Legionella/Pneumococcal urinary antigens: Pneumococcal positive.
summary.imaging_results: CXR: Dense RLL consolidation with parapneumonic effusion. CT Chest: RLL consolidation with loculated pleural effusion. Thoracentesis: Exudative, pH 7.12, glucose 30, LDH 1200 - complicated parapneumonic effusion.
summary.hospital_course: Sepsis protocol initiated with aggressive fluid resuscitation and vasopressors (norepinephrine) in ICU for 48 hours. IV ceftriaxone and azithromycin started. Chest tube placed for complicated parapneumonic effusion with tPA/DNase instillation. Transferred to floor day 3. Vasopressors weaned. AKI resolved with hydration. Chest tube removed day 6. Clinical improvement with defervescence.
summary.discharge_diagnosis: Severe community-acquired pneumonia (Streptococcus pneumoniae) with sepsis, Complicated parapneumonic effusion, Acute kidney injury (resolved), COPD exacerbation
summary.discharge_medications: Amoxicillin 1g TID x 5 more days, Fluticasone/vilanterol 100/25 daily, Prednisone 40mg x 5 day taper, Metoprolol 25mg BID, Furosemide 20mg daily, Metformin 500mg BID (hold if creatinine rises), Lisinopril 10mg daily
summary.follow_up_instructions: Pulmonology 2 weeks with repeat CXR. PCP 1 week. Pneumococcal vaccine update (PCV20 if not received). Check creatinine in 1 week.

summary.chief_complaint: Blurred vision, numbness in legs, and difficulty walking for 1 week
summary.history_of_present_illness: 35-year-old female presenting with subacute onset optic neuritis (blurred vision OD with pain on eye movement), bilateral lower extremity numbness and paresthesias, and gait ataxia. Reports episode of transient left arm numbness 6 months ago that resolved spontaneously.
summary.past_medical_history: No significant PMH. Prior episode of transient neurological symptoms 6 months ago (not evaluated)
summary.physical_examination: BP 120/76, HR 72. Visual acuity: OD 20/100, OS 20/20. RAPD positive OD. Color vision impaired OD. Decreased proprioception and vibration sense bilateral lower extremities. Romberg positive. Tandem gait impaired. DTRs brisk 3+ in LEs. Positive Babinski bilateral.
summary.lab_results: CSF: WBC 8 (lymphocytic), Protein 55, Glucose 62, Oligoclonal bands: 6 (not present in serum), IgG index: 1.2 (elevated). AQP4 antibody: negative. MOG antibody: negative. B12: normal. NMO-IgG: negative. JC virus: negative.
summary.imaging_results: MRI Brain: Multiple periventricular, juxtacortical, and infratentorial T2/FLAIR lesions with at least 3 enhancing lesions. MRI Spine: Cervical cord lesion at C3-4 with enhancement.
summary.hospital_course: McDonald criteria met for MS diagnosis with dissemination in space and time. IV methylprednisolone 1g daily x 5 days for acute relapse. Visual acuity improved to 20/40 OD. Gait improved with PT. Started on disease-modifying therapy (ocrelizumab). Neuropsychological baseline assessment scheduled. MS nurse educator consultation. Vitamin D supplementation initiated.
summary.discharge_diagnosis: Relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis, Acute optic neuritis, Transverse myelitis
summary.discharge_medications: Ocrelizumab infusion scheduled (first dose in 2 weeks), Prednisone 60mg oral taper over 2 weeks, Vitamin D3 5000 IU daily, Gabapentin 300mg TID for neuropathic pain, Baclofen 10mg TID for spasticity
summary.follow_up_instructions: Neurology/MS clinic 2 weeks. Ophthalmology 1 month with OCT. Infusion center for ocrelizumab. Annual MRI Brain and Spine surveillance. MS support group referral.

summary.chief_complaint: Gross hematuria, urinary retention, and lower abdominal pain for 3 days
summary.history_of_present_illness: 78-year-old male with BPH presenting with painless gross hematuria with clots, acute urinary retention, and suprapubic discomfort. Reports progressive LUTS over the past year with nocturia x5, weak stream, and incomplete emptying. PSA trend: 4.2 → 8.5 → 15.2 over 2 years.
summary.past_medical_history: BPH, Hypertension, Atrial fibrillation on anticoagulation, Chronic kidney disease Stage 3a
summary.medications_on_admission: Tamsulosin 0.4mg daily, Finasteride 5mg daily, Warfarin 5mg daily (INR 2.8), Metoprolol 50mg BID
summary.physical_examination: BP 155/88, HR 80 irregular. Suprapubic distension and tenderness. DRE: Firm, nodular prostate, estimated 80g. No rectal masses. Foley catheter draining grossly bloody urine with clots.
summary.lab_results: Creatinine: 1.8, PSA: 15.2 ng/mL, free PSA: 8%, INR: 2.8, Hemoglobin: 9.8 (was 12.5), Urinalysis: gross blood, WBC 15/hpf. Urine cytology: atypical cells present.
summary.imaging_results: CT urogram: Enlarged prostate (85cc), bilateral hydroureteronephrosis, irregular bladder wall thickening at trigone. MRI prostate: PI-RADS 5 lesion in right peripheral zone (2.3cm).
summary.hospital_course: CBI initiated for clot evacuation. Warfarin held, bridged with enoxaparin. Transrectal ultrasound-guided prostate biopsy: Gleason 4+3=7 (Grade Group 3) adenocarcinoma in 8/12 cores. Bone scan: No metastases. Staging: cT2c N0 M0 (localized high-risk). Urology and radiation oncology discussed treatment options. Patient elected radical prostatectomy with robotic assistance (scheduled). Hematuria resolved.
summary.discharge_diagnosis: Prostate adenocarcinoma Gleason 7 (4+3), Gross hematuria secondary to prostate cancer, BPH with urinary retention, AKI secondary to obstruction
summary.discharge_medications: Tamsulosin 0.4mg daily, Finasteride 5mg daily, Warfarin 5mg daily (resume), Metoprolol 50mg BID, Ferrous sulfate 325mg daily
summary.follow_up_instructions: Urology 2 weeks for pre-op. Robotic prostatectomy scheduled in 4 weeks. Hold warfarin 5 days pre-op. Cardiology clearance. Pelvic floor PT referral pre-op.

summary.chief_complaint: Palpitations, tremor, weight loss, and heat intolerance for 2 months
summary.history_of_present_illness: 44-year-old female presenting with 20-lb unintentional weight loss despite increased appetite, palpitations, fine tremor, heat intolerance, and anxiety over 2 months. Reports new onset bulging eyes and reddened shins. Family history of thyroid disease.
summary.past_medical_history: No significant PMH. FHx: Mother with Graves disease
summary.physical_examination: BP 150/60 (wide pulse pressure), HR 128 (AF on ECG), Temp 37.4°C. Proptosis bilateral (Hertel 24mm OU). Lid lag and lid retraction. Diffusely enlarged non-tender thyroid (3x normal), thyroid bruit present. Fine tremor hands. Brisk reflexes. Pretibial myxedema bilateral shins. Proximal muscle weakness.
summary.lab_results: TSH: <0.01 (suppressed), Free T4: 5.8 (elevated, normal 0.8-1.8), Free T3: 12.5 (elevated), TSI: 450% (markedly elevated), Anti-TPO: positive. CBC normal. LFTs: mildly elevated ALT 68. Calcium: 10.8.
summary.imaging_results: Thyroid uptake scan: Diffusely increased uptake (55%, normal 10-30%). Echocardiogram: Mild LVH, EF 55%, no valvular disease. CT orbits: Bilateral enlargement of extraocular muscles, increased orbital fat.
summary.hospital_course: Diagnosed with Graves disease with thyroid storm (Burch-Wartofsky score 50). ICU admission for monitoring. Started methimazole 20mg TID, propranolol 60mg Q6H, hydrocortisone 100mg Q8H, cholestyramine for T4 binding. Heart rate controlled. Ophthalmology consult for Graves ophthalmopathy - started selenium supplementation. Converted to NSR with rate control. Transitioned to oral medications.
summary.discharge_diagnosis: Graves disease with thyroid storm, Graves ophthalmopathy (moderate-severe active), New-onset atrial fibrillation (converted to NSR), Pretibial myxedema
summary.discharge_medications: Methimazole 30mg daily, Propranolol 80mg BID, Prednisone 40mg daily (ophthalmopathy, taper per ophtho), Selenium 200mcg daily, Calcium/Vitamin D, Apixaban 5mg BID (for AF)
summary.follow_up_instructions: Endocrinology 2 weeks with TFTs. Ophthalmology 1 week. CBC at 2 weeks (agranulocytosis screening). Discuss definitive therapy (RAI vs surgery) at follow-up. No smoking.
